name: Microsoft Spends a Bunch on Patents — But Is It Worth It?
description: USA Today's Kevin Maney calculates Microsoft spent ~$9.5M per patent in 2003 — far more than IBM ($2.5M), HP, or Sun. Maney questions whether $5B+ annual R&D yields breakthrough products. Peter Kastner of Aberdeen Group defends Microsoft Research as underappreciated. The piece foregrounds the disconnect between research output and commercial mega-hits beyond Windows and Office.
